CHARTS / BASKETS
% RETURN, REBASED · EOD 2026-06-25
DEFENSIVES LEADCyclicals -3.0% vs defensives on the month
Rotation ReadCitrini · claude

AI hardware leads everything else this month, and software is paying for it. Semis returned +25.9% and AI infra +27.9%, while SaaS fell -6.2% and software -4.7%. The Mag 7 basket lost -5.5% for the month even as XLK gained +8.07%. The money is moving into chips and servers, away from the applications layer. Quantum added +32.2% for the month from a flat ytd base of -0.7%: speculative capital moving into hardware ahead of proved fundamentals.

The defining split is XLK versus XLC. Technology sits +21.4% ytd with a z-score of +3.26 against SPY, in the top 1% of its 1-year range. Communication services, the home of Alphabet and Meta, sits -14.3% ytd at a z-score of -3.34, in the bottom of its 1-year range. These two used to trade together. They don't. The market is pricing AI spend into the chip and server supply chain and walking away from the platform layer where monetization is still unproven. Industrials at +5.96% and materials at +4.2% for the month add a capex-cycle bid: spending is landing in the real economy. XLE's collapse of -10.08% for the month sits alongside that, cleanly separating oil from the rest of the cyclical complex.

International breaks along the same fault line. Taiwan (EWT/SPY z-score +3.16, in the top of its 1-year range, ytd +55.4%) and Korea (EWY/SPY z-score +2.63, in the top of its 1-year range, ytd +96.2%) are running pure semiconductor exposure. Emerging markets are outperforming developed markets (EEM/EFA z-score +2.95, in the top of its 1-year range), but China is not part of the trade. MCHI/SPY sits at a z-score of -2.71 in the bottom of its 1-year range, and FXI trails EM by -33.6% ytd. The EM outperformance runs through Taipei and Seoul.

AI hardware dominates while the platform software layer reprices lower: the same sector, two opposite trades

BULL

XLK at a z-score of +3.26 (in the top 1% of its 1-year range), SPHB in the top of its 1-year range, and semis up +25.9% confirm the AI capex cycle is accelerating. Industrials at +5.96% and banks at +8.3% for the month say the economy is absorbing that spending.

BEAR

Mag 7 down -5.5% for the month and SaaS down -16.0% ytd signal that AI monetization at the platform level is being discounted. XLC in the bottom of its 1-year range (-14.3% ytd, z-score -3.34) is a heavy index weight working against the rally's durability.

TELL

XLC/SPY at a z-score of -3.34 is the tell. A stabilization and reversal there means the rally is broadening beyond hardware into the full AI stack. A continued decline means you have a narrow hardware squeeze with index concentration risk building.

⚠ SPHB/SPY in the top of its 1-year range (z-score **+2.99**) and SPHB/SPLV at **+2.76** signal strong risk appetite and broad cyclical leadership vs XLE fell **-10.08%** for the month, a drawdown that typically reads as risk-off or recession pricing in the commodity complex: Both coexist because the high-beta strength is driven by AI and tech hardware stocks, not commodity-linked ones. Energy's selloff reflects oil supply dynamics specific to crude. Treat XLE as a macro warning only if credit spreads or TLT confirm it. Trust SPHB for positioning.
⚠ XLK/XLP at a z-score of **+2.69** (in the top 2% of its 1-year range) and XLK/XLU at **+2.85** read as a clean cyclicals-beat-defensives regime vs XLC/SPY at **-3.34** (in the bottom of its 1-year range, **-14.3%** ytd) has a growth sector collapsing alongside defensive laggards, breaking the cyclical/defensive framework: XLC's collapse is sector-specific: ad platform and streaming derating on their own monetization cycle, independent of the risk regime. XLK/XLP is the correct read for macro regime inference. XLC's underperformance is idiosyncratic. Trust XLK/XLP.

MTUM/SPY at a z-score of +4.26 (in the top of its 1-year range, ytd +22.5%) is the most extreme reading in the table and the clearest mean-reversion risk in the factor space. Momentum chasing is at maximum.

Fade it after semis roll over; until then, tighten stops on momentum-factor ETFs. XLK/SPY at +3.26 (in the top 1% of its 1-year range) and XLK/XLU at +2.85 (in the top 2% of its 1-year range) are stretched but confirmed by industry flows, so they read as trend rather than reversion.

SOXX/SMH at +3.08 (in the top of its 1-year range, ytd +15.3%) says breadth within semis is expanding, which reinforces the trend read for the whole complex. SPHB/SPY at +2.99 (in the top of its 1-year range) and SPHB/SPLV at +2.76 (in the top 1% of its 1-year range) are consistent with the hardware bid and are not obvious fades.

The cleaner reversion candidates sit in international: EWT/SPY at +3.16 (ytd +55.4%) and EWY/SPY at +2.63 (ytd +96.2%) have priced an enormous amount of semiconductor cycle strength. A single negative data point from TSMC on orders snaps both.

Within EM, EWZ/EEM at -2.80 (in the bottom of its 1-year range) flags Brazil as the weakest link inside the EM outperformance story. On the short side, XLC/SPY at -3.34 (ytd -14.3%) and MCHI/SPY at -2.71 (ytd -22.4%) are in confirmed downtrends.

You don't fade either without a catalyst.
